commit.message:
Random assembly animation: rows appear in shuffled order

Each row gets a deterministic-random delay so the tower
assembles chaotically instead of linearly bottom-to-top.
Duration increased to 0.7s for smoother appearance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(ux): setup wizard and startup banner (v0.2.1) (#8)

* feat(ux): setup wizard and startup banner (v0.2.1)

Setup wizard (macroa/cli/wizard.py):
- Auto-triggers on first launch when no API key is configured
- Interactive 3-step flow: API key → name → model preferences
- Writes to ~/.macroa/.env so config persists across projects
- `macroa setup` command re-runs wizard at any time
- Friendly validation, clear explanations, safe defaults throughout

Startup banner (macroa/cli/renderer.py):
- Replaced bare one-liner with a Rich two-column panel
- Left column: version, greeting (uses MACROA_USER_NAME), activity summary
- Right column: live model stack showing current NANO/HAIKU/SONNET/OPUS IDs
- Footer quick-reference for key commands

Settings (macroa/config/settings.py):
- Added user_name field (MACROA_USER_NAME env var)
- Now loads ~/.macroa/.env before project .env (override=False)
  so wizard config works without touching any project files

Tests: 13 new tests in tests/test_wizard.py (183 total, all passing)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(config): correct .env priority order — project beats wizard defaults

With override=False, the FIRST load_dotenv call wins for each variable.
The original code loaded ~/.macroa/.env first, causing user-level wizard
settings to silently win over project .env values.

The attempted fix using override=True on the project .env call also caused
real shell env vars to be overwritten (override=True is unconditional).

Correct approach: load project .env first, wizard defaults second, both
with override=False. Priority is then: shell > project .env > wizard.

Added two regression tests that verify both the project-beats-wizard
ordering and the shell-beats-both guarantee.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: re-trigger after actions allowlist update

* fix(lint): clean up ruff violations in wizard and renderer

- Remove unused imports (time, Columns, Path, MagicMock, pytest)
- Fix import ordering (I001) in main.py and test_wizard.py
- Remove f-prefix from strings with no placeholders (F541)
- Drop unused local variable `log` in test (F841)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Potential fix for code scanning alert no. 64: Empty except

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <62310815+github-advanced-security[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* Potential fix for code scanning alert no. 65: Empty except

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <62310815+github-advanced-security[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* Potential fix for code scanning alert no. 66: Empty except

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <62310815+github-advanced-security[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* Potential fix for code scanning alert no. 67: Empty except

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <62310815+github-advanced-security[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(wizard): quote all .env values to prevent hash-comment truncation

Unquoted values written to .env are silently truncated at ' #' because
python-dotenv treats that as a comment delimiter. For example:

  MACROA_USER_NAME=Alice #1   →  read back as "Alice"

Fix: wrap every written value in double quotes and escape any embedded
double quotes. This makes the round-trip safe for arbitrary user input.

Added two regression tests that verify ' #' and embedded '"' survive
a write → dotenv_values() round-trip intact.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(audit): use COUNT(DISTINCT session_id) for accurate lifetime session count

The banner's session count was computed by fetching the 500 most recent
audit entries in Python and counting unique session_id values in that
sample. For users with >500 entries this silently undercounted sessions.

Fix: add a COUNT(DISTINCT session_id) query to AuditLog.stats() and read
the value from there in the renderer — one SQL call, correct for any
log size, no Python-side sampling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(wizard): preserve custom model settings on rerun

Three related bugs when rerunning the setup wizard:

1. _step_models returned {} on "keep" → _write_env wrote a file with no
   MACROA_MODEL_* lines → custom model settings silently deleted on rewrite
2. Table showed hardcoded defaults instead of the user's current values,
   so users couldn't see what they already had configured
3. Advanced path used hardcoded defaults as prompt baseline, so a user
   reverting a tier to the hardcoded default would also lose that entry

Fix: read current env values as the effective baseline for both display
and prompting. "Keep" now returns the full current config dict so every
tier is written explicitly — nothing is ever implicitly dropped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

feat: make all jj output JSON by default for Claude Code (#16)

Convert all jj output commands across 6 plugins to use structured JSON
templates instead of human-readable text:

- jj log: use -T 'json(self)' for full Commit object as JSON
- jj diff: use custom template with .escape_json() for file list as JSON
- jj bookmark list: use -T 'json(self)' for CommitRef as JSON  
- jj op log: use -T 'json(self)' for Operation as JSON

Commands without template support (jj status) and visual output
(jj diff --stat) are left unchanged. Custom templates (-T 'bookmarks',
-T 'if(empty, ...)') are also preserved.

Requires jj >= 0.31.0 (json() function).

Optimize app for buttery smooth performance

iOS wrapper:
- Set webView opaque with matching background (avoids compositing)
- Disable horizontal bounce, lock horizontal scroll
- GPU-accelerated CSS injected: translateZ(0), will-change on interactive elements
- Snappier tap feedback (scale 0.97, 150ms cubic-bezier)

channel renders correctly.

commit.message:
fix: render omission_candidates using correct field name (missing_frame)

The renderer was looking for om.get("text") / om.get("description") but
the actual schema fields are missing_frame and reason_expected. This caused
all omission bullet points to render as blank even when reviewers returned
valid omission data. Now reads missing_frame first with fallback chain,
and appends reason_expected after a dash when present.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix enemy sprite loading issue - move Enemies.init after sprite loading

- Enemies were being initialized before sprites loaded, causing fallback to colored boxes
- Now enemies initialize after loadSprites() completes, ensuring sprites are available
- This should fix the 'mobs are just boxes' issue

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Implement full incident response playbook tool

FastAPI backend with rule engine that cross-references incidents against
multi-jurisdictional breach notification laws, evaluates exemptions, and
generates step-by-step response plans with deadlines. Streamlit frontend
with incident forms, interactive timeline (Gantt/table), document generation
(.docx templates), jurisdiction browser, and LLM-powered admin extraction.
10 jurisdictions seeded, 52 tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: incident-response-playbook
repository.description: Cybersecurity incident response tool that generates step-by-step legal response plans with jurisdiction-specific notification obligations, deadlines, exemptions, and downloadable notice documents.

commit.message:
Add daily granularity with period toggle to PR Velocity chart (#109)

* Enforce all test layers in CI and add Definition of Done

Add Jest CI workflow (jest.yml) running lint + unit/integration/component
tests on every PR and before deploy. Update deploy.yml to gate on both
Jest and Playwright in parallel. Add test:ci script, exclude worktree
paths from Jest, fix outdated Playwright status in docs, and codify a
Definition of Done checklist in CLAUDE.md.

Closes #96 (branch protection setup tracked separately)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove lint step from jest.yml to fix CI failure

ESLint 10.0.2 resolved via npm ci is incompatible with
eslint-plugin-react from eslint-config-next (getFilename error).
Lint was never in CI before — remove it from the Jest workflow
and track the ESLint compatibility fix separately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Pin ESLint to 9.39.3 and restore lint step in CI

The caret range allowed npm ci to resolve ESLint 10.0.2, which is
incompatible with eslint-plugin-react from eslint-config-next. Pin
to exact version 9.39.3 and restore the lint step in jest.yml.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix inaccurate 'serial' claim in test:ci comment

The --ci flag does not force serial execution — it changes snapshot
behavior and disables watch mode. Remove misleading description.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add "Previous Month" date range option to time tracking

Adds a "PM" button to the time range selector so users can review the
full previous month's data — useful on the 1st of the month when MTD
resets and 14d doesn't cover all of last month.

Closes #98

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add source column to snapshot tables, fix scheduler_log schema,
add has*Today() dedup guards to API routes, switch Docker to named volumes.

- Add least-privilege permissions (contents: read) to jest.yml and deploy.yml

* Add cache-first read layer for team PR and time tracking APIs

Check SQLite for today's snapshot before hitting ADO/7Pace. Fall back
to stale cache (up to 7 days) when upstream APIs fail. Responses now
include a data_source field indicating origin (live/cache/cache-stale).

- Add DataSourceMeta type and extend TeamSummaryApiResponse + TeamTimeData
- Create lib/cache-utils.ts with isSnapshotFresh, buildDateRange, findMissingDates
- Add 5 read functions to lib/snapshots.ts (getTeamSnapshot, getTeamSnapshotRange,
  getTimeSnapshot, getTimeSnapshotRange, checkTeamCoverage)
- Use _team_response_ sentinel row for full TeamTimeData cache in time_tracking_snapshots
- Wire cache-first + stale-fallback logic in both API routes
- Add tests for cache-utils, snapshot reads, and API cache behavior (326 pass)

Closes #67

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address CodeRabbit review feedback on cache layer

- Make cache range-aware: verify cached period.days matches requested
  range before serving from cache (both PR and time tracking routes)
- Fix corrupt JSON handling: safeJsonParse null now returns cache miss
  instead of wrapping null in a truthy object; range readers skip
  corrupt rows and try next-most-recent
- Enrich checkTeamCoverage return type to { covered, total, complete }
- Add tests: range mismatch falls through to live, malformed JSON
  treated as cache miss (332 tests pass)

- Trend charts (PR velocity, alignment score) on Team tab, open by default
- Sprint-over-sprint comparison cards with delta badges
- Weekly hours chart on Time Tracking tab
- Nightly scheduler via node-cron + instrumentation.ts (2 AM UTC)
- Manual scheduler trigger at /api/admin/scheduler/run

- Data freshness indicator in dashboard header
- Trend API routes: /api/trends/team-pr, sprint-comparison, time-hours

- Lower chart threshold to show data with any snapshots available

granularity=weekly for backward compat. Chart includes Daily/Weekly
segmented toggle that re-fetches only trend data.

v2.6.0: save error chats to database for debugging failed API calls

When an API call fails during execution, the chat (messages, variables,
VM state, statements) is now saved to the database with error details,
so users can inspect what was sent. VM no longer calls sys.exit(9) on
error — raises VMExecutionError instead, letting ChatManager persist
the state before reporting the failure.

commit.message:
Fix cursor lag on Safari: switch left/top to GPU-composited transform

Safari doesn't composite left/top animations on the GPU — only transform.
Switching the cursor rAF loop to transform: translate() eliminates the
lag. Also:
- Lerp factor 0.35 → 0.5 for snappier tracking on all browsers
- Scale animation moved into rAF loop (smooth lerp, no CSS transition)
- will-change: transform hints Safari to keep cursor on its own layer

docs: add empty system prompt to codex command for speed (#1300)

## Summary

- Add `-c system_prompt=''` to the Codex example command to skip the
default system prompt, reducing tokens and latency for commit message
generation

Follow-up from #1295 — @max-sixty asked about using empty system prompts
for speed.

**opencode**: Does not currently support disabling the system prompt.
The system prompt is always injected, and even the plugin system has a
safety fallback that restores the original if emptied. There are open
feature requests
([#1242](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/issues/1242),
[#1894](https://github.com/sst/opencode/issues/1894),
[#5005](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/issues/5005)) but nothing
available yet.

**Codex**: Has an undocumented `system_prompt` config key that can be
overridden via `-c system_prompt=''`. There are feature requests for
dedicated `--system-prompt` flags
([#6023](https://github.com/openai/codex/issues/6023),
[#11588](https://github.com/openai/codex/issues/11588)) but not yet
merged.

Co-authored-by: worktrunk-bot <254187624+worktrunk-bot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: exclude worktree dirs from find in fmt recipes (#15)

The find commands in fmt and fmt-check crawl into .worktrees/ and
.claude/ directories, which may contain full repo copies from git
worktrees. This causes formatting to run on duplicate files, roughly
doubling execution time.

Add -path exclusions for .worktrees/ and .claude/ to all four find
commands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: correct query bugs and optimize hot paths in sqlite_vec storage (PR #557) (#563)

Manually rebased from chriscoey/mcp-memory-service#557 after conflicts with
#558 and #562 prevented auto-merge.

Bug fixes (P0):
- Soft-delete leaks: recall() (both semantic and time-based paths),
  get_memories_by_time_range(), get_largest_memories(), and
  get_memory_timestamps() (both branches) were missing deleted_at IS NULL
- Score formula: recall() used 1.0 - distance for cosine distance, but
  cosine distance ranges [0, 2]; changed to max(0.0, 1.0 - (distance / 2.0))
  to correctly map to [0, 1]
- Tag parsing: get_largest_memories() used json.loads() to parse tags, but
  tags are stored as comma-separated strings; changed to split(",")

Performance optimizations (P1):
- Batch access metadata: retrieve() now persists access metadata in one
  executemany call instead of N individual UPDATE+COMMIT round-trips
- Hybrid search dedup: retrieve_hybrid() replaced O(n x m) nested-loop
  deduplication with O(n+m) dict-based merging and replaced N+1 individual
  embedding fetches with a single batch query

Tests:
- 9 new regression tests covering all fixed methods

Co-authored-by: Henry Krupp <doobidoo@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Chris Coey <coey.chris@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

用 baseScale 縮回顯示大小，FLIP 動畫與 pan 計算一併更新。

feat: add /wireframe skill for ASCII wireframe generation (#5)

* fix: add scope constraint to prevent Stitch from adding unspecified UI elements

- Add mandatory Scope Constraint block to enhance-prompt skill (Step 3E + output template)
- Remove hardcoded modelId: GEMINI_3_PRO from prototype skill

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /wireframe skill for ASCII wireframe generation

Create new /wireframe skill to generate ASCII wireframes instantly from spec.md and DESIGN.md. This enables rapid layout validation before high-fidelity Stitch prototyping.

**Skill details:**
- No external API calls — Claude generates wireframes directly
- Reuses prototype skill's screen-plan.md format for compatibility
- 4-step flow: prerequisite check → scenario analysis → wireframe generation → completion report
- Includes full drawing rules and component inventory tables
- Integrates seamlessly into design pipeline: /spec → /extract-design → /wireframe → /prototype

**Kanban-todo example:**
- Generated screen-plan.md with 3 light-mode wireframes covering all 6 scenarios
- 01-board-default: 3-column Trello-style board with drag/drop and add card UI
- 02-card-edit-delete: inline edit mode + delete confirmation dialog
- 03-search-filter: active search/filter with result filtering
- Dark mode (scenario 6) noted as layout-equivalent with color mapping

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message: Use purple ghost icon in window title bars and HTML instead of emoji - Set app-level Icon to TrayIcon64 so Windows/Linux show the purple ghost in the taskbar and window title bar - Replace ghost emoji with of the actual icon in Settings and Wizard HTML windows - Copy icon to frontend/ so Wails serves it to the webview Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6

feat: add DEFAULT_PROVIDER env var to select default model provider

When multiple providers are configured (OpenRouter, OpenAI, Anthropic,
CF AI Gateway), this env var lets users explicitly choose which one
serves as the primary model instead of relying on the hardcoded
priority order. All providers with valid API keys are now registered
independently, enabling model switching in the UI regardless of which
is the default.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(analytics): exclude CMS, add per-user filter, auto-layout flow map

- Skip page_view tracking for /cms/* paths (admin activity)
- DB functions filter out CMS paths, accept optional p_user_id
- User picker dropdown in flow map (shows tracked users)
- Auto-layout algorithm: force-directed positioning based on actual
  data instead of hardcoded positions for 27 potential pages
- Stats summary bar (pages visited, total transitions, most visited)
- Hover tooltips with view/user counts on nodes
- get_tracked_users() DB function for user list
- Migration 328
